failingTest: getTagDef should match case-insensitively (to be added in tags.test.ts)
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — case-insensitive comparison in getTagDef()
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase and mixed-case test cases
fixApproach: In getTagDef(), normalize both the lookup key and the stored tag name via .toLowerCase() before comparing. This makes all lookups case-insensitive in one place. Since isTagSupportedByProvider() delegates to getTagDef(), it inherits the fix for free. Existing lowercase lookups are unaffected since lowercasing an already-lowercase string is a no-op.
steps: In apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts, modify getTagDef() to compare t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase() instead of t.tag === tagName
In apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts, add test cases for uppercase ('PROSODY'), mixed-case ('Break'), and lowercase ('emphasis') lookups, plus a negative case ('nonexistent')
Run npm run test to confirm all tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive so SSML tags like 'PROSODY' and 'Break' resolve correctly.
